g.title: argument
g.text: Ambedkar on caste as enforced endogamy

g.title: cluster_9c3f450b
g.text: Caste arises by endogamy imitation and exclusion

g.title: cluster_d447c7e9
g.text: Caste is endogamy imposed on exogamy

g.title: cluster_f1b7eb00
g.text: Origin and spread of caste explained

g.title: var_761ba197
g.text: (claim: Caste arises by endogamy imitation and exclusion)

g.title: claim_049ff556
g.text: Exogamy is the original and strongly enforced marriage rule

g.title: claim_23973221
g.text: Creating caste in India consists in superimposing endogamy

g.title: claim_40f9987b
g.text: A group can preserve endogamy only if it maintains numerical
parity between marriageable men and marriageable women

g.title: claim_486bbdbc
g.text: Endogamy is the only characteristic peculiar to caste and
therefore constitutes the essence of caste.

g.title: claim_70d68916
g.text: Practices such as sati, enforced widowhood, celibacy for
widowers, and girl marriage function to manage surplus women
and surplus men within the group.

g.title: claim_761ba197
g.text: Caste arises by endogamy imitation and exclusion

society into enclosed groups through the superimposition and
maintenance of endogamy, originating with Brahmins and

g.title: claim_8fc46f38
g.text: Surplus widows and surplus widowers threaten endogamy
because they are likely to marry outside the group if not

g.title: claim_9ce59f9e
g.text: Groups excluded by existing castes or expelled for violating

g.title: claim_ca401598
g.text: Non-Brahmin groups imitated Brahmin endogamy and its
supporting customs, so imitation spread caste formation from

g.title: claim_d21f07a2
g.text: These practices preserve and perpetuate endogamy by
preventing surplus spouses from breaking the group’s marital

g.title: claim_d3cdc835
g.text: India has a deep cultural unity despite ethnic
heterogeneity, so its population forms a larger homogeneous

g.title: claim_ff8e9859
g.text: These customs are observed most strictly among Brahmins and
appear in non-Brahmin castes in derivative form, indicating
that Brahmins first enclosed themselves as a caste.
